name: TRUST.md Specification
description: An open specification for documenting AI agent trustworthiness. Like README.md for trust.

required_sections.name: Identity
required_sections.description: Agent name, builder, platform, creation date

required_sections.name: Capabilities
required_sections.description: What the agent can do — verified by the builder

required_sections.name: Verification
required_sections.description: Current verification status and layers completed

trust_dimensions.identity_integrity: Does the agent consistently represent itself honestly?
trust_dimensions.operational_reliability: Does the agent complete tasks correctly and consistently?
trust_dimensions.security_compliance: Does the agent follow security rules and protect sensitive data?
trust_dimensions.memory_continuity: Does the agent maintain context and remember important information?
trust_dimensions.boundary_respect: Does the agent stay within its defined boundaries?
trust_dimensions.transparency: Is the agent honest about its limitations and mistakes?

verification_layers.description: Builder confirms the profile is accurate
Third-party or automated testing confirms claims
Community validates through usage and feedback

scoring_guidance: Be honest. Vivienne's score is 69, not 100. A perfect score is a red flag. Every agent has weaknesses — document them.
hosting: Place TRUST.md at your project root. Place trust-profile.json at /.well-known/trust-profile.json for machine discovery.
license: Open specification. Use it, extend it, contribute back.
